function: hb-buffer-verify.cc:buffer_verify_unsafe_to_break(hb_buffer_t*, hb_buffer_t*, hb_font_t*, hb_feature_t const*, unsigned int, char const* const*)
what_input_feature: A structurally valid sfnt/font input (consistent table header at offsets 0x00-0x0f, e.g. the '01 46 ... kern' shape) that shapes into multiple glyphs and sets HB_GLYPH_FLAG_UNSAFE_TO_BREAK on at least one glyph, making info[end-(forward?0:1)].mask & HB_GLYPH_FLAG_UNSAFE_TO_BREAK true at line 124.
why_winner_satisfies: The winner reaches line 124 with T=540 F=0 (the OR-arm true side fires), and value_profile's CMP_MAP distance buckets reward inputs whose CMP operands approach matching the font-table magic/length checks, steering the corpus toward valid sfnt headers that survive shaping; winning seeds carry the consistent '01 ... kern' header at offsets 0x00-0x0f.
why_loser_doesnt: naive blocks 8/10 trials and at line 124 reaches only T=0 F=180 (mask bit never set); loser seeds are short/garbled (6-byte and 35-byte inputs with no coherent table header) so they never shape into a buffer whose glyphs carry the UNSAFE_TO_BREAK flag.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's per-CMP distance-bucket feedback (CMP_MAP gradient) gives partial credit for getting closer to satisfying the font-format integer comparisons, so it climbs toward valid headers that produce shaped buffers with UNSAFE_TO_BREAK glyphs; naive has no such gradient and stalls on the all-or-nothing checks.

what_input_feature: The same concentrated magic/table-tag literals at offsets 0x00-0x0f, reached via a Hamming/prefix-distance gradient over the multi-byte comparison rather than by direct substitution.
why_winner_satisfies: vpc adds the CMP_MAP gradient: a near-match input that gets the table-tag comparison closer than any seen pair adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved, ratcheting toward the 'loca'/magic literal that unlocks glyf->get_extents.
why_loser_doesnt: plain cmplog has no CMP_MAP bucket feedback, so partial-prefix progress on the multi-byte comparison earns no coverage credit and is discarded; cmplog resolved only 1/10 trials versus vpc's 10/10.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP records a matching-prefix-length bucket per comparison PC, rewarding inputs that approach the table-tag literal byte-by-byte; combined with I2S in vpc this gives a gradient cmplog alone lacks.

what_input_feature: A 4-byte feature-record tag equal to the ASCII literal 'size' (73 69 7a 65) reaching the FeatureParams::sanitize tag dispatch, satisfying the exact 32-bit equality tag == HB_TAG('s','i','z','e').
why_winner_satisfies: The winner-resolving seed (value_profile_cmplog) contains the byte run 73 69 7a 65 ('size') at offset 0x39, so the true branch at line 688 is taken and resolution occurs; the value_profile CMP_MAP gradient feedback rewards inputs that are Hamming-closer to the 'size' constant, steering the corpus toward the full 4-byte match.
why_loser_doesnt: The loser-blocking cmplog seeds carry 'GSUB'/'GPOS' tags (47 53 55 42 / 47 50 4f 53) but never the 'size' tag; at the blocker the loser side takes the false branch (T=0 F=54) on every reached execution, so without the gradient signal cmplog stalls before assembling the exact 'size' constant.
mechanism_attribution: Both fuzzers already have I2S (cmplog); the only added technique in the winner is value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient feedback, which buckets partial-match (Hamming/prefix) distance to the compared constant as new coverage, giving an intermediate climbing signal toward the 32-bit 'size' equality that bare cmplog lacks.

what_input_feature: A FeatureList record whose tag bytes spell 'size' (0x73 0x69 0x7a 0x65) reaching OT::Feature::sanitize, so that the line-813 equality closure->tag == HB_TAG('s','i','z','e') holds and the true side of the line-812 compound gate is entered.
why_winner_satisfies: The winner-resolving seed (value_profile_cmplog, trial=2) contains the literal byte run 73 69 7a 65 ('size') at offset 0x39, satisfying the closure->tag == HB_TAG('s','i','z','e') equality, and the per-role overlay marks lines 815-826 (the size-feature fixup body) as winner-only [W].
why_loser_doesnt: None of the five loser-blocking seeds (cmplog) contain the byte run for 'size'; they carry GSUB/GPOS/GSHB table tags instead, so closure->tag never equals HB_TAG('s','i','z','e') and the line-812 gate stays false (L shows T=0 F=54 at line 812).
mechanism_attribution: Both cmplog and value_profile_cmplog already have the input-to-state substitution mutator, so comparison-solving via substitution is NOT the differentiator. The winner adds value_profile: the CMP_MAP gradient buckets the Hamming/prefix distance of the 4-byte tag comparison at line 813, rewarding inputs that progressively match more bytes of 'size' and guiding the search to the exact literal that cmplog-only fails to assemble within budget.

what_input_feature: Same GPOS-tag table-directory requirement, but reachable incrementally: input bytes at the tag offsets need only progressively approach the 'GPOS' 4CC for the comparison to register as closer-to-matching.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile resolves 9/10 with avg hitcount 21383 vs loser 2810; its CMP_MAP rewards inputs whose tag bytes are Hamming-closer to the expected table tag, ratcheting toward the valid 4CC at offsets 0x0c-0x0f where winners show k/G, e/P, r/O, n/S.
why_loser_doesnt: naive's loser seeds keep arbitrary low-entropy bytes at the tag offsets (0x0c L bytes include 20( ), 80, 70(p), 01) with no feedback rewarding partial-match progress, so they never converge on the tag and block 8/10.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient buckets the operand-distance of the table-tag comparison; each input that brings the tag bytes Hamming-closer to 'GPOS' adds a new bucket and is preserved, giving a smooth path to the magic 4CC that naive's binary edge feedback cannot follow.

what_input_feature: Same GPOS table-tag / sfnt structure requirement, where the input progressively approaches the required tag and header field values that gate the table_index==1 layout context.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile beats naive (resolved 9/10 vs 2/10) by preserving intermediate inputs whose CMP operands are closer to the required tag/header constants, building a gradient toward the structurally valid font.
why_loser_doesnt: naive has no CMP-distance feedback, so partial-match progress toward the tag/header constants is not preserved as corpus and the structure is never assembled.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP records operand-distance buckets (Hamming / matching-prefix length) and a new closer-matching bucket is preserved as corpus, giving a gradient toward the tag and header constants naive cannot follow.

what_input_feature: A font blob whose kern-table region (offsets ~0x10-0x2f) holds concrete non-padding sub-table offset/length fields rather than 0x20 filler, so kerning-table parsing advances far enough to evaluate has_machine_kerning and return false.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner seeds carry varied structural bytes at the table region (e.g. 0x10=00, 0x18=01/35, 0x1a=00/35) instead of uniform 0x20; value_profile's CMP_MAP distance feedback rewards inputs whose parsed table-length/offset comparisons move closer to satisfying the kern-table validity checks, preserving partial progress as corpus.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser (naive) seeds are dominated by 0x20 padding across 0x06-0x2f, so the kern sub-table fields never form a valid offset chain; naive has no distance signal to incrementally close the gap and stays blocked on the true side.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP records a Hamming/prefix-distance bucket per CMP; an input whose kern-parse comparisons get closer to passing adds a new bucket and is kept, giving a gradient toward the structurally-valid font that naive lacks.

function: hb-ot-shaper-indic.cc:initial_reordering_consonant_syllable(hb_ot_shape_plan_t const*, hb_face_t*, hb_buffer_t*, unsigned int, unsigned int)
what_input_feature: Same program-side condition: a KANNADA-script syllable spanning >=3 glyphs (start+3<=end). The axis distinguishing the two fuzzers is the value_profile (CMP_MAP gradient) feedback, not the byte feature itself.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves the branch in 8/10 trials (avg hitcount 2) vs cmplog's 1/10; its winning seeds (Seed 1/2, vpc trial 1) carry the same long glyph runs ('tttttttt', repeated 'G 0c 00 00') that build >=3-glyph syllables, and vpc adds CMP_MAP gradient buckets that reward progress toward branch-relevant comparison states cmplog cannot see.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog (loser in this pair too) blocks in 8/10 trials and never takes the True side at L479 (T=0 F=15); lacking the CMP_MAP gradient feedback it gets no partial-progress reward for inputs that approach the >=3-glyph syllable length and stays stuck on short inputs.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog wins over cmplog via the CMP_MAP gradient: the Hamming/value-profile comparison feedback gives a corpus-add signal for inputs that make incremental progress toward the comparison/length states gating the branch, feedback that bare cmplog lacks. The differentiating winner technique is the CMP_MAP gradient.

function: hb-ot-shaper-indic.cc:initial_reordering_consonant_syllable(hb_ot_shape_plan_t const*, hb_face_t*, hb_buffer_t*, unsigned int, unsigned int)
what_input_feature: Same structural requirement (old-spec Indic syllable with a Halant glyph after the base consonant taking the true side of line 678), reachable when the value-profile gradient feedback rewards inputs whose category/codepoint bytes approach the H-after-base layout.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves 9/10 (avg hitcount 2); its CMP_MAP gradient buckets give partial credit for byte sequences that move toward matching the comparison at line 678, retaining intermediate inputs that the loser discards.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog (no value_profile) resolves 1/10 and blocks 9/10 with branch hitcount 0; lacking the CMP_MAP gradient it gets only all-or-nothing edge feedback and its I2S substitutions disrupt the H bytes, so it never retains the near-miss inputs needed to reach the true side.
mechanism_attribution: vpc beats cmplog and the differentiating technique is value_profile, so the enabling mechanism is the CMP_MAP gradient: Hamming/prefix-distance buckets reward partial progress toward the H-category comparison at line 678, letting vpc climb to the true side where plain edge feedback (cmplog) stalls.

function: hb-ot-shaper-indic.cc:initial_reordering_syllable_indic(hb_ot_shape_plan_t const*, hb_face_t*, hb_buffer_t*, unsigned int, unsigned int)
what_input_feature: Same indic_symbol_cluster syllable requirement; value_profile_cmplog additionally gets CMP_MAP-bucket feedback that rewards partial progress in the comparisons feeding the categorization path.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves 8/10 vs cmplog's 1/10 over the same branch; its instrumentation adds CMP_MAP gradient buckets on top of cmplog, giving a Hamming-distance signal cmplog lacks.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog has only edge-bucket feedback and the I2S stage; with no gradient signal it stays blocked 9/10 at hitcount 0 while vpc advances.
mechanism_attribution: The added technique vpc has over cmplog is the value_profile CMP_MAP gradient (Hamming/prefix-distance feedback buckets), which rewards inputs closer to category-relevant comparison operands, enabling vpc to reach indic_symbol_cluster where bare cmplog cannot.

what_input_feature: The same VPre-bearing Myanmar consonant syllable, but reached under value-profile gradient feedback that rewards partial progress toward the categorized record bytes (the 0x10 second-byte / d7 run structure).
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog adds CMP_MAP gradient buckets on top of cmplog; the winner seeds (all fuzzer=value_profile_cmplog) reach the branch with hitcount 6 and resolve 9/10, showing the gradient feedback retains intermediate inputs approaching the VPre-producing record layout.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog has the same I2S mutator but NO CMP_MAP gradient feedback, so intermediate inputs that are byte-wise closer to a VPre record earn no new-coverage reward and are discarded; it blocks 8/10 with avg duration 17.80h and hitcount 1.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog's CMP_MAP Hamming/prefix-distance gradient gives credit for partial matches on the comparison values that gate category classification, letting the corpus climb toward a VPre-categorized glyph; cmplog without that gradient gets no such intermediate reward.

what_input_feature: Same program-side condition: a Myanmar consonant syllable carrying a VPre left-matra glyph that triggers the POS_PRE_M assignment at line 236, satisfying the line-280 true side.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog adds the CMP_MAP gradient (Hamming/prefix-distance) feedback on top of cmplog's I2S, so partial progress toward the category-byte values that decode to VPre is rewarded as new coverage; it resolves 9/10 with the VPre/POS_PRE_M chain reached, and its winning seeds are dense 0x84 0x10 / 0xd7 category runs.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog has I2S but lacks the CMP_MAP gradient; with no reward for partial byte-distance progress it stalls on the same category-byte gate (blocked 8/10, hitcount 1) and never assembles a VPre-bearing record, so line 280 true side stays at L T=0.
mechanism_attribution: Pair 2 delta is value_profile; both fuzzers have I2S, so the discriminating technique is the CMP_MAP gradient feedback that vpc has and cmplog does not. The gradient rewards incremental Hamming progress toward the multi-byte category values that decode to a VPre glyph, letting vpc cross the gate cmplog stalls on.

function: hb-ot-shaper-use.cc:find_syllables_use(hb_buffer_t*)::{lambda(hb_pair_t<unsigned int, hb_glyph_info_t const&>)#1}::operator()(hb_pair_t<unsigned int, hb_glyph_info_t const&>) const
what_input_feature: Same structural requirement (ZWNJ glyph followed by a non-ignorable non-mark glyph in the buffer), reached by gradually accreting the multi-byte tag/category bytes that select the right USE categories.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog adds CMP_MAP gradient buckets that reward partial progress toward the byte patterns selecting ZWNJ + following non-ignorable glyph categories, so it accretes the needed structure and resolves the false side in 8/10 trials.
why_loser_doesnt: Plain cmplog has only edge-bucket feedback plus I2S substitution and no Hamming/prefix gradient; it gets no reward for partial matches, the I2S substitutions overwrite progress, and it stays blocked in 9/10 trials.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog's CMP_MAP gradient feedback gives a smooth Hamming-distance signal that incrementally guides the input toward the byte combination producing the ZWNJ-plus-non-ignorable glyph structure, which plain cmplog lacks. This is the CMP_MAP gradient mechanism.

what_input_feature: The same numeral-cluster category run; the differentiator is that the run is approached incrementally — partial category prefixes that the CMP_MAP gradient can reward as it climbs toward the accepting transition for case 14.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog adds the value_profile CMP_MAP gradient on top of cmplog's edge+I2S signal, giving partial-progress feedback over the comparison values in the state-machine key lookups (lines 966-967, '_keys[0] <= ... <= _keys[1]'), so near-miss category runs are retained and refined; vpc resolves 8/10.
why_loser_doesnt: plain cmplog has only edge-bucket feedback plus I2S; it gets no gradient credit for category runs that are close-but-not-accepting, so partial progress toward case 14 is discarded and the branch stays blocked 10/10 (hitcount 0).
mechanism_attribution: The winner vpc differs from the loser cmplog by exactly the value_profile technique (CMP_MAP gradient / Hamming-prefix distance feedback). The gradient over the trans-key comparison values lets vpc retain incrementally-closer inputs that converge on the numeral-cluster transition. Mechanism enabling the winner = CMP_MAP gradient.

what_input_feature: The same multi-glyph syllable with a USE(R)-category leading glyph (length>1), reached when intermediate equality/category comparisons on the glyph-category path are partially satisfied.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog adds CMP_MAP gradient buckets on top of cmplog, so partial progress toward info[start].use_category()==USE(R) is rewarded as new coverage; the winner resolves 9/10 with blocked duration only 5.60h vs the loser's 23.60h, indicating the gradient feedback steers it onto the USE(R) record faster.
why_loser_doesnt: Cmplog has the identical I2S mutator and the same edge-bucket feedback but NO CMP_MAP gradient bucket, so it gets no partial-credit signal toward the equality gate and stalls (resolved=0/10, blocked=10, dur 23.60h); the only technique difference between the two arms is value_profile.
mechanism_attribution: CMP_MAP gradient: the value_profile feedback distinguishing vpc from cmplog records Hamming/prefix proximity of the use_category comparison operands, rewarding inputs that get closer to USE(R), which guides corpus growth onto the gate that plain cmplog cannot sense.

what_input_feature: Same structural requirement (a halant-category glyph inside a reordered USE syllable), but reached here under value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient feedback which rewards partial progress toward the comparison/category state.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves 9/10, blocked only 1, average blocked duration 8.50h with prob_div 0.90 vs cmplog. The CMP_MAP gradient buckets give intermediate-distance credit so the corpus retains seeds that approach the needed glyph-category state.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog (no value_profile) blocks 10/10 for 23.60h with hitcount 0; lacking the Hamming/prefix-distance gradient feedback, it gets no intermediate credit toward the structural state and stalls.
mechanism_attribution: The only technique delta between the winner value_profile_cmplog and loser cmplog is value_profile, i.e. the CMP_MAP gradient feedback that rewards partial comparison progress and keeps near-miss seeds. Enabling technique = CMP_MAP gradient.

what_input_feature: A glyph sequence where the current glyph codepoint is 0x0905 and the next glyph codepoint (buffer->cur(1)) equals one of the 16-bit case constants 0x093Au/0x093Bu/0x093Eu/0x0945u (and the following lines 0x0946..0x0957), reached only after the buffer script is decoded as HB_SCRIPT_DEVANAGARI.
why_winner_satisfies: The winner-resolving seed begins with 05 09 00 00 3b 09, i.e. little-endian codepoint 0x0905 followed by 0x093B, exactly the (0x0905, 0x093Bu) pair that drives the inner switch into the blocker true side; value_profile took the branch in 9/10 trials.
why_loser_doesnt: The loser seeds reach the 0x0905 outer case but their second codepoint never lands on a listed vowel: e.g. seed 2 is 05 09 00 00 05 09 (next codepoint 0x0905, not a vowel) and seed 3 stacks 0x093A bytes loosely (40 09 3a 3a...) without forming a clean 0x0905-then-vowel adjacency, so the inner switch falls through (F=6) and naive resolved in only 1/10.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile intercepts the integer CMP that compares buffer->cur(1).codepoint against each 16-bit case constant and writes a Hamming-distance bucket into CMP_MAP; an input whose second codepoint is closer to a target vowel adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved, giving a gradient that walks the 16-bit value onto an exact case constant. naive lacks CMP interception so must hit the exact 16-bit value by blind havoc.

what_input_feature: Three consecutive buffer codepoints encoding 0x0930 (RA) at the current glyph, then 0x094D (virama) at cur(1), then 0x0907 at cur(2), with idx+2 < count so the third glyph exists. In the harness input these appear as little-endian 32-bit glyph words 30 09 00 00 / 4d 09 00 00 followed by the third codepoint slot.
why_winner_satisfies: The winner-resolving seed begins 30 09 00 00 4d 09 00 00 06 00 00 00 — the first glyph word is 0x0930 (entering case 0x0930) and the second word is 0x094D (the virama required by cur(1)), supplying the leading two operands of the three-way conjunction that the loser never satisfies; the winner takes the true side (L94 W T=1 F=14 vs L T=0 F=149).
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds carry unrelated bytes at those slots (e.g. 19 09 00 00, 00 00 00 00, sn-hant/zh- ASCII tag strings) so they never co-locate 0x0930, 0x094D and 0x0907 at adjacent glyph positions; across 10 loser trials case 0x0930 stays false (L94 L T=0 F=149) and the branch is blocked for 18.80h.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds CMP_MAP gradient buckets on top of cmplog's I2S. The blocker is a conjunction of three exact 32-bit codepoint equalities; CMP_MAP's Hamming/prefix-distance feedback rewards inputs that match MORE of the three operands partially (e.g. getting cur()==0x0930 and cur(1)==0x094D before cur(2)==0x0907), giving an intermediate-credit gradient that cmplog (edge-bucket feedback only) cannot see, so vpc climbs the multi-equality gate that cmplog stalls on.

what_input_feature: A glyph stream whose current codepoint equals the exact 16-bit Gurmukhi constant 0x0A72, entered only after the outer switch has already selected the HB_SCRIPT_GURMUKHI arm; the loser must produce this same 0x0A72 codepoint that gates the case label at line 144.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves the branch in all 10 trials (avg 3.70h) and the per-role overlay marks the Gurmukhi arm (lines 130-163) plus the blocker case 0x0A72u as [B]/hit on the winner side, with the inner switch at line 145 taken True 7 times for W versus only 1 for L.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog blocks the branch in 8/10 trials (avg 20.10h, hitcount 0); having only I2S without the value_profile CMP_MAP gradient it cannot incrementally close in on the exact 0x0A72 codepoint, so it almost never satisfies the case label.
mechanism_attribution: Both involved fuzzers carry the same input-to-state comparison-solving mutator, so that technique is held constant; the only technique delta is value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient buckets, which reward Hamming/prefix progress toward the 0x0A72 codepoint comparison and let the winner climb to the exact match the loser cannot reach by comparison-solving alone.

what_input_feature: A glyph buffer whose script resolves to HB_SCRIPT_GURMUKHI and that contains an adjacent codepoint pair where buffer->cur()==0x0A72 and buffer->cur(1) is one of {0x0A3F,0x0A40,0x0A47}, so that `matched` becomes true at the blocker line 162.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog (winner) uniquely reaches `case 0x0A72u:` (line 144 W T=1 vs L T=0) and takes the true side of the blocker (line 162 W T=1 vs L T=0), resolving in all 10 trials, because its CMP_MAP gradient feedback rewards inputs that progressively match the two-codepoint constraint comparison.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog (loser) blocks in all 10 trials and never enters the 0x0A72 arm (line 144 L T=0); it iterates the Gurmukhi loop heavily (line 131 L T=300) but only its edge-bucket feedback fires, giving no reward for partial progress toward the exact codepoint pair, so it never assembles the matched pair.
mechanism_attribution: The only technique delta between the two involved fuzzers is value_profile: both carry the same input-to-state CMP substitution mutator, but only value_profile_cmplog adds CMP_MAP gradient buckets. The Hamming/prefix-distance value-profile feedback makes near-miss codepoint comparisons (e.g. one byte off from 0x0A72 / 0x0A3F) register as new coverage, steering the corpus toward the exact matched vowel pair that cmplog's edge-only feedback cannot distinguish from any other non-matching glyph.

what_input_feature: Same structural requirement (codepoint 0x0B05 at buffer->cur()), reached by gradient-guided convergence of the integer compare buffer->cur().codepoint == 0x0B05 toward equality.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile resolves 8/10. Multiple winning seeds are stored by fuzzer=value_profile and carry 05 0b at the codepoint position (seeds d07f30cd, d13c11f6 contain `05 0b`). The codepoint==0x0B05 compare in the case dispatch feeds CMP_MAP; an input whose codepoint operand is Hamming-closer to 0x0B05 adds a new distance bucket and is preserved, climbing the gradient to an exact match.
why_loser_doesnt: naive has no CMP_MAP feedback, so a near-miss codepoint (e.g. 0x0B0F at `0f 0b`, one nibble off) produces no new signal and is discarded; naive blocks 8/10 with avg hitcount 0 on the branch versus value_profile's nonzero hit.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP records operand-distance buckets for the codepoint comparison, giving a smooth gradient toward the literal 0x0B05 that plain edge-coverage naive cannot follow.

what_input_feature: Same parsed-buffer requirement: HB_SCRIPT_MALAYALAM script with current glyph codepoint 0x0D12 (case 0x0D12u true side). The codepoint equality is the comparison that must be satisfied.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves 8/10 with hitcount 3; adding the CMP_MAP gradient (Hamming/prefix-distance feedback) over cmplog gives partial-credit feedback as the buffer codepoint approaches 0x0D12, letting it climb toward the MALAYALAM script and codepoint match its winning seeds (structured fonts with GSUB/GPOS/GSTB tags) satisfy.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog blocks 8/10 with hitcount 0; lacking the CMP_MAP gradient it gets no graded signal toward the codepoint comparison, and its I2S substitution alone does not steer the multi-stage script-then-codepoint dispatch, so the true side stays unhit.
mechanism_attribution: The value_profile delta is the CMP_MAP gradient: vpc's Hamming/prefix-distance CMP_MAP buckets give graded feedback toward the codepoint==0x0D12 comparison, which cmplog (edge-bucket feedback only) lacks. This is CMP_MAP gradient, not I2S.

what_input_feature: Input bytes that decode (via the harfbuzz fuzzer harness) to a Sinhala-script buffer whose current glyph codepoint equals U+0D91 (little-endian bytes 91 0d ...), so the equality switch on buffer->cur().codepoint dispatches to case 0x0D91u.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile's CMP_MAP records how close each integer CMP came to matching, so the codepoint==0x0D91 comparison gets a distance-bucket gradient that preserves near-miss inputs and climbs toward the exact match; winner-resolving seeds carry the literal 91 0d codepoint bytes (e.g. value_profile seed 3 has 91 0d at offsets 0x08 and 0x0c).
why_loser_doesnt: naive only has bare edge-counter feedback; the 0x0D91 switch arm is one discrete value among many Sinhala cases, so naive must blindly hit the exact 16-bit codepoint with no proximity signal and stays blocked all 10 trials.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP per-(PC, operand-distance bucket) feedback gives a Hamming/prefix-distance gradient on the codepoint==0x0D91 integer comparison, rewarding near-miss codepoints and letting the fuzzer hill-climb to the exact value naive cannot find.

what_input_feature: A numeric token shaped like 0x<hexdigit>: byte '0' (0x30) at the number start immediately followed by 'x' (0x78) or 'X' (0x58), then a hex digit. The gate requires zNum[1] to equal 'x'/'X' after zNum[0]=='0'.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog winning seeds carry literal 0x prefixes inside SELECT expressions, e.g. seed 06e09a37e739e806 'SELECT-0x85E' and seed 239da0d3632856b0 'SELECT -0x11CCC1...'; the byte diff shows W concentrates 0x30('0') at offset 0x08 and 0x78('x') at offset 0x09 exactly where L does not.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog loser seeds are all-decimal numeric expressions like 'SELECT.5-5/55555' (seed 0c1d027799a90a48) and 'SELECT.5E1-55' (seed 05b666d01ebcac10); they hold 0x35('5') runs at the relevant offsets and never place 'x'/'X' after a '0', so zNum[1]=='x' is always false.
mechanism_attribution: Both fuzzers in this pair already have the cmplog input-to-state substitution mutator, so that technique is held constant; the only added technique in the winner is value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient. The 'x'/'X' single-byte comparison and the surrounding hex-token compares produce Hamming/prefix-distance buckets that reward seeds whose number byte is creeping toward the 0x form, giving the winner intermediate credit that pure edge feedback (cmplog) cannot see, so it climbs to the literal 0x token.

source_line: case OP_Concat: {           /* same as TK_CONCAT, in1, in2, out3 */
what_input_feature: A well-formed SQL statement whose expression tree contains a string concatenation, i.e. the literal '||' operator (bytes 7c 7c) between two text operands inside a SELECT, so the parser emits an OP_Concat opcode that the VDBE reaches at /src/sqlite3/bld/sqlite3.c:86705.
why_winner_satisfies: All three winner-resolving seeds are syntactically valid SELECT statements containing '||': seed 1 has 'SELECT 11-0133=1...3=1||3=13' with 7c 7c at offset 0x32, and seeds 2 and 3 carry 'SELECT'''L''''''' followed by '||' (7c 7c) at offsets 0x11-0x12; the BYTE DIFF confirms W concentrates on 7c(|)x2 at offsets 0x11 and 0x12 and on the SELECT keyword bytes 53(S)/45(E)/4c(L)/45(E)/43(C)/54(T) at offsets 0x00-0x05.
why_loser_doesnt: The naive loser-blocking seeds are non-parsing byte garbage (e.g. 'T 1; c BINARY' or '``g..;......y' or high-byte blobs) with no SELECT keyword and no '||' operator at any offset, so they never compile to a program containing OP_Concat and OP_Concat stays at T=0 in the loser column.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile adds a CMP_MAP keyed by (PC, operand-distance bucket) over intercepted CMPs in the SQL tokenizer/parser; inputs that match more of the 'SELECT' keyword prefix or get a closer byte match on the '||' token produce a new closer CMP_MAP bucket and are kept as corpus, letting value_profile climb the gradient toward syntactically valid concatenation SQL that naive's plain edge feedback cannot assemble by luck.

source_line: case OP_ShiftLeft:              /* same as TK_LSHIFT, in1, in2, out3 */
what_input_feature: A syntactically valid SQL statement (a SELECT with an expression) that lexes a shift token (<< or >>), so the parser emits an OP_ShiftLeft/OP_ShiftRight VDBE opcode and the interpreter dispatches into the shift case at line 86940. This requires correct multi-byte SQL keyword and operator tokens, not just any bytes.
why_winner_satisfies: All winner-resolving seeds begin with the literal keyword 'SELECT' and carry repeated structured token runs ('+:L', '+#L'), i.e. a real SQL expression that reaches the VDBE; the winner takes the true branch (T=4) and only the winner enters the case body (line 86950 fires T=4 for W, T=0 for L).
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds are short malformed fragments ('SELEEC .`', '""/"" ', '``g..;') that never lex into a SELECT with a shift expression; the loser's coverage shows T=0 at the OP_ShiftLeft case and the whole VDBE opcode dispatch row is T=0 F=40 for L versus T=4 for W.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient feedback rewards inputs whose CMP operands move closer to matching (matching-prefix length for the string CMPs in SQL's keyword/token tables, Hamming-distance bucket for integer CMPs). This lets value_profile incrementally assemble the valid SELECT-with-shift syntax that the parser requires, whereas naive (and cmplog without value_profile) get no partial-match credit and stall on the all-or-nothing keyword/operator comparisons. Both decisive pairs add exactly value_profile, isolating CMP_MAP gradient as the enabling technique.

source_line: case OP_ShiftRight: {           /* same as TK_RSHIFT, in1, in2, out3 */
what_input_feature: Input bytes that the SQL tokenizer accepts as a valid expression containing a bit-shift operator ('>>' = 0x3e 0x3e, or '<<'), so the code generator emits an OP_ShiftRight/OP_ShiftLeft opcode that the VDBE then dispatches via the shared 'case OP_ShiftRight' at sqlite3.c:86941. Reaching it requires a SELECT-like prefix plus a two-operand shift sub-expression.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds are well-formed SELECT statements carrying shift/comparison operator bytes: Seed 1 'SELECT 1*9988878...>9=...' and Seed 3 'sELECT' followed by repeated '>>' (0x3e 0x3e) runs. The BYTE DIFF shows the winner concentrates the readable prefix S/E/L/E/C/T and '>'(0x3e) bytes at the operand offsets, and OP_ShiftRight is hit T=6 on the winner side.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking naive seeds are short, syntactically broken byte soup dominated by ';'(0x3b), '+'(0x2b) and high bytes (e.g. Seed 1 'i;;;;;;; . Z;ELE', Seed 2 '#1;.. S..#$'); they never form a parseable shift expression, so the parser/codegen never emits the opcode and OP_ShiftRight stays T=0 (blocked) for the loser.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient feedback rewards inputs whose intercepted CMPs (the tokenizer/keyword matching and operator-character comparisons) get progressively closer to matching valid SQL keyword/operator literals; the distance-bucket signal preserves partial-prefix SELECT/shift-operator inputs that naive's plain edge feedback cannot ratchet toward, letting value_profile assemble a parseable '>>' expression.

source_line: case OP_Lt:               /* same as TK_LT, jump, in1, in3 */
what_input_feature: Same program-side requirement: a parseable SELECT with a '<' comparison that compiles to and dispatches OP_Lt. The byte-level requirement is a multi-byte match against the parser's token comparisons (the 'SELECT' keyword and operator tokens), which value_profile reaches via incremental partial-prefix progress feedback.
why_winner_satisfies: The value_profile decisive subject resolves 10/10 with avg hitcount 506 on the branch vs the loser's 189; its corpus reaches OP_Lt (L87187 T side) because value_profile's CMP_MAP preserves inputs that get progressively closer to matching the parser's keyword/operator comparisons.
why_loser_doesnt: Naive's loser-blocking seeds never match the multi-byte keyword comparison (their offset-0 bytes are 'i', ' ', 'd5' rather than 'S'), and naive has no distance feedback to reward a near-match, so it stays blocked (8/10 blocked, branch True side T=0 for loser).
mechanism_attribution: value_profile writes each CMP into a CMP_MAP keyed by (PC, operand-distance bucket); an input whose operand pair is closer to matching than any seen before adds a new bucket and is preserved as corpus. This Hamming/prefix gradient guides the corpus toward the exact 'SELECT'/operator bytes needed to compile an OP_Lt, which naive's edge-only feedback cannot reward.

what_input_feature: SQL text that parses as a three-part qualified name 'X.Y.Z' (two dots) reaching the TK_DOT case in resolveExprStep, so lookupName is called with zDb != 0; reaching the true side of `if( zDb )` at line 97837.
why_winner_satisfies: The winner-resolving seed is 'SELECT M.L. .L.4 ...' with multiple '.' (0x2e) separators producing dotted X.Y.Z tokens; per-role overlay shows the winner alone hits lines 97838-97861 (the zDb body) and line 98387 lookupName call after the TK_DOT path, and the blocker count shows W T=1 vs L T=0 at L97837.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds are 'SELECT CC........' / 'SELECT+SSE++++++' with no two-dot qualified-name structure, so the parser yields TK_ID (or no dotted name) and lookupName is called with zDb==0; the overlay marks the zDb-body lines as not-hit for L and the blocker shows L T=0 F=20.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient feedback rewards inputs whose CMP operands get closer to producing the dotted-name token structure: each near-match on the byte comparisons in the tokenizer/parser adds a new CMP_MAP distance bucket, preserving partial-progress seeds so the rare two-dot qualified-name layout is assembled incrementally. Naive/cmplog (without CMP_MAP) lack this gradient and only rarely stumble onto the structure via havoc.

what_input_feature: A SQL statement whose column expression names a table/column that survives lookupName's SrcList scan so cntTab becomes non-zero before line 97941 (e.g. a qualified reference like 'M.L' whose table prefix matches a SrcList table name via sqlite3StrICmp), making zDb==0 && zTab!=0 && cntTab==0 false.
why_winner_satisfies: The winner-resolving seed begins 'SELECT M.L' — a dotted table.column token. Per-role coverage shows the winner alone walks the zDb/table-matching block (97847-97861) and the rowid path at 98025, and at the blocker line 97941 it takes the false side (T=0 F=1 for W), i.e. cntTab incremented so the trigger-fallback guard is false.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds carry degenerate column tokens ('SELECT CC...', 'SELECT+SS...') that do not match any SrcList table, so cntTab stays 0; the loser exclusively reaches the double-quoted-string / error path at 98116-98162 and at line 97941 takes only the true side (T=0 F=20 for L) — the trigger/upsert fallback.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient feedback: the sqlite3StrICmp calls comparing the input column/table token against schema names emit Hamming/matching-prefix distance buckets; an input whose token is closer to a real table/column name produces a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved, letting value_profile climb toward a token that fully matches and increments cntTab, which naive (edge-only) cannot gradient toward.

what_input_feature: A SQL statement whose column reference is a three-part schema-qualified name (X.Y.Z, e.g. db.table.column) so that lookupName is called with a non-NULL zDb; the name must fail to resolve so cnt!=1 and control reaches the error block, where 'if( zDb )' (98156) is then true.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seed carries repeated dotted multi-part qualifier structure (e.g. '.112/1111', 'E.E.E' runs), and the d=2 dispatch shows the winner taking case TK_DOT (L98355 T=1) which builds zDb/zTable/zColumn before calling lookupName; at d=1 the winner takes 'if( zDb )' true (L97837 T=1) and the blocker 'if( zDb )' true (L98156 T=1).
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds are flat single-token or repeated-letter payloads ('SELECT bbLLLL...', 'SELECT bbbbb') with no nested X.Y dotted qualifier; the loser never builds zDb (d=1 L97837 T=0 F=20, blocker L98156 T=0 F=20) — it routes through the unqualified column path (case TK_ID, L98354 T=20).
mechanism_attribution: The pair delta is value_profile: both fuzzers share the same input-to-state comparison-substitution mutator, but only value_profile_cmplog adds the CMP_MAP gradient (Hamming/prefix-distance buckets). Reaching the db-qualified branch requires the input to first survive several near-miss string comparisons (sqlite3StrICmp on db names, schema-name matching) and to emit a syntactically valid dotted reference; the CMP_MAP gradient rewards incremental Hamming progress toward those multi-byte comparison targets, letting vpc assemble the X.Y.Z structure that plain cmplog (edge-bucket feedback only) cannot incrementally climb toward.

what_input_feature: The SQL input must contain a dotted column/table reference (an ID '.' ID token sequence such as M.L), so the parser emits a TK_DOT expression node; the switch in resolveExprStep then takes the TK_DOT case arm at line 98355 and descends into the pExpr->op!=TK_ID else-block (lines 98366-98386).
why_winner_satisfies: The value_profile winner seed begins 'SELECT M.L. .L.4' with literal '.' (0x2e) bytes separating identifier characters (M, L), forming the ID.ID dotted-reference structure that parses to a TK_DOT node; value_profile resolved 9/10 trials and was the only seed to take the true side (W=1 true at L98355).
why_loser_doesnt: The naive loser seeds contain no dotted-reference structure: they are runs of '+' (0x2b), digits/commas ('SELECT ( 111111,'), or '~'/'<' fillers, so the parser never builds a TK_DOT node and the switch falls through to TK_ID / TK_VARIABLE / TK_LT arms instead; naive blocked 9/10 with 0 hits on the true side.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient feedback rewards inputs whose intercepted token/character comparisons (e.g. the byte comparison that classifies an expression operator as the '.' / TK_DOT token) move closer to matching, preserving partial-progress seeds that naive's pure edge-coverage discards; this Hamming/prefix-distance bucketing lets value_profile incrementally assemble the rare ID.ID dotted syntax that naive cannot stumble onto with havoc bytes alone.

what_input_feature: Same structural feature (operator-joined integer-literal SELECT expression) reaching codeInteger's true branch, approached incrementally by closing CMP-operand distance.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile resolved 10/10 with avg hitcount 112 on the branch, far above the loser's 1, indicating it repeatedly reaches and takes the codeInteger codegen path.
why_loser_doesnt: naive stays blocked 8/10 at the same branch with hitcount 1, never accumulating the literal-arithmetic structure needed.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP records a Hamming/prefix-distance bucket per CMP; an input that brings an integer-literal comparison closer to matching adds a new bucket and is preserved, providing a gradient toward the byte values the codegen branch requires.

what_input_feature: A SQL statement parsed into an expression tree whose code generation reaches line 103567 and supplies an integer-CMP operand that must equal/approach the constant on the true side; the winner builds this via an arithmetic expression with operators (*, <<, %) over multi-digit numeric literals.
why_winner_satisfies: The value_profile winning seed is 'SELECT 1*$1<< 11119099%.19999666...' — a deeply nested arithmetic expression with operators and long numeric literals, exactly the integer-comparison-rich code path that CMP_MAP distance feedback can climb; the branch true side fires (W: T=1 F=6 at L103567) and avg hitcount on the branch is 22.
why_loser_doesnt: The naive loser seeds are either 'vacuum' / 'vacuum;;...' (a different statement type that never codes this expression comparison) or trivial 'SELECT 1;;+...' / 'SELECT?19CCCCCC...' forms lacking the arithmetic operand structure; the loser takes only the false side (L: T=0 F=892 at L103567) and never hits the branch (avg hitcount 0).
mechanism_attribution: value_profile intercepts integer CMPs and writes a (PC, distance-bucket) CMP_MAP entry; an input whose arithmetic operand lands closer to the true-side constant adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved, giving a Hamming/prefix gradient toward the exact value naive must hit by blind luck.

what_input_feature: A SELECT statement whose result expression is a chain of relational/arithmetic operators (the winning seed is `SELECT \t22&22%22>>22...`), so sqlite3ExprCodeTarget is invoked on an operator-node Expr that drives the codegen path through line 103568 on its TRUE side, rather than a trivial integer literal.
why_winner_satisfies: The winning seed (value_profile_cmplog, 51 bytes) is `SELECT` followed by `22&22%22>>22222...` — a long expression with `&`, `%`, `>` operators feeding the expression-codegen walk, and the branch is hit (avg hitcount on branch winner=8, loser=0).
why_loser_doesnt: The loser-blocking seeds are bare/short queries (`SELECT 1;`, `SELECT 1:C:...`) with no operator-expression chain, so sqlite3ExprCodeTarget's operator arm at 103568 never takes its TRUE side (loser hitcount=0).
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient feedback: each integer/relational CMP inside the expression evaluation writes a (PC, operand-distance bucket) entry; an input whose operand pair is closer to matching adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus, letting value_profile incrementally build the operator-laden expression that reaches the codegen arm. naive and plain cmplog lack the CMP_MAP distance feedback and never get the gradient reward, so they stall on trivial SELECTs.

what_input_feature: An input that the SQL parser accepts deeply enough to call sqlite3TwoPartName and take its true branch at line 111036 — i.e. byte sequences forming valid SQL keyword/identifier tokens (e.g. a statement beginning 'vacuum'/SELECT-like text and ';'-delimited clauses) that satisfy the chained integer/string comparisons in the lexer and parser CMPs leading to the blocker.
why_winner_satisfies: Both winning-resolving seeds begin with recognizable SQL token bytes ('vacuum') and ';'-delimited clause structure, and the blocker line is hit only by W (W=T=2 F=2 vs L=T=0 F=20). The fuzzers that resolve it are exactly the two carrying value_profile feedback; value_profile preserves intermediate inputs whose lexer/parser CMP operands are progressively closer to matching, building a gradient toward the full token sequence the parser requires.
why_loser_doesnt: The loser-blocking seeds are short, malformed fragments ('SELEEC .`', '""/""', random byte runs) that fail early lexer/parser comparisons and never reach the blocker's true side (L hits L111036 only T=0 F=20). naive and cmplog lack the CMP_MAP distance bucket, so they receive no smooth 'getting closer' reward and cannot ratchet toward the multi-comparison parser path.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient: each intercepted integer/string comparison in the SQLite lexer/parser writes an operand-distance (Hamming / matching-prefix) bucket; an input whose CMP operands are closer to matching adds a new bucket and is kept as corpus, letting the fuzzer climb the chain of comparisons gating sqlite3TwoPartName. naive (no CMP_MAP) and cmplog (CMP buffer feeds I2S substitution, not distance feedback) lack this gradient, so adding value_profile is the decisive delta in both pairs.

what_input_feature: A SELECT statement whose result columns include a qualified-wildcard reference (e.g. 'LLTL.*' — an identifier followed by '.' and '*'), so selectExpander reaches the true side of the wildcard/column-name expansion comparison at line 134117 rather than the degenerate false side.
why_winner_satisfies: The winner-resolving seed is 'SELECT LLTL.*. .' — a SELECT with a qualified '.*' wildcard column reference; in the divergent-branch trace W takes the true side at the blocker (L134117 W: T=1 F=0) and progresses past it (W reaches L134120/L134132/L134154/L134252 with T=1), reflecting that the column-expansion path executed.
why_loser_doesnt: The naive loser seeds are degenerate result lists like 'SELECT ( 111111,' and 'SELECT+SE+...' / 'SELECT ~~~~~44<4' with no '<ident>.*' wildcard token; in the trace L reaches L134112 (T=223) and L134114 but takes the false side at the blocker (L134117 L: T=0 F=223), never satisfying the comparison the true branch needs.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP records (PC, operand-distance-bucket) for the integer/string CMPs inside selectExpander's name-resolution / wildcard match; as havoc mutants drift their column-reference bytes toward a matching prefix, each closer operand pair opens a new CMP_MAP bucket and is retained, giving a gradient that climbs toward the exact comparison the true branch gates on. naive's plain edge-counter feedback gets no such intermediate reward and only flips the branch by luck.

what_input_feature: An input that lexes/parses into a VACUUM statement reaching sqlite3Vacuum and satisfying the line-138735 condition on its true side; the seed begins with the 'vacuum' keyword plus additional structured trailing bytes (';', token bytes) that complete the parse path into the VACUUM reduce action.
why_winner_satisfies: Both winner-resolving seeds begin with the literal 'vacuum'/';vacuum' keyword and carry substantial structured trailing bytes (e.g. seed aa08bf50: ';vacuum\n\nEL.;...' 139 bytes; seed 8cb368bd: 'vacuum''m''d''''''' 115 bytes). value_profile's CMP_MAP records operand-distance buckets for the integer/string comparisons in the lexer/parser, so partial-match inputs that are progressively closer to a valid VACUUM-reduce parse are preserved as corpus, climbing the gradient to take the true branch (winner avg hitcount on branch=4 / =2).
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds either are the bare 6-byte 'vacuum' (seed 84fc92d4) or 'vacuum' plus short noisy suffixes (seeds a68a9e5a, c8a20ace, f035a5db) that take the false side of L138735 (loser hitcount on branch=0; L138735 shows T=0 F=4). naive and cmplog lack the CMP_MAP distance feedback, so they get no partial-progress credit toward the parser comparisons that gate the true branch and remain blocked ~9/10 trials for 15-18h.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient: each intercepted CMP writes a (PC, operand-distance bucket) entry, and an input producing a closer-than-seen comparison pair adds a new bucket and is kept. This Hamming/prefix-distance feedback lets value_profile incrementally build the parser comparisons gating sqlite3Vacuum's true branch; naive and cmplog (the losers) lack CMP_MAP, which is the single technique delta in both decisive pairs.

what_input_feature: An input whose leading bytes spell the SELECT keyword followed by expression tokens (dotted-list / column-reference structure), reaching the deep expression-parse reduction in yy_reduce at 157646.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile winner seeds begin with the literal bytes 53 45 4c 45 43 54 20 ('SELECT ') and continue with dotted-list expression tokens, so they drive resolveExprStep (15 fires in W, 0 in L) and hit the blocker true side 11 times.
why_loser_doesnt: naive loser-blocking seeds do not start with SELECT (they begin with 'T ', backticks, 'E', ';'), never enter the expression-resolution path, and take the blocker false side 61/61.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP records operand-distance (matching-prefix length) for the tokenizer/keyword integer and string CMPs; an input one byte closer to spelling SELECT and the subsequent expression tokens adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved, so VP ratchets bytes toward the valid SELECT-expression prefix that naive cannot assemble by blind havoc.

what_input_feature: A byte sequence that parses into the specific SQL grammar production whose reduce action is dispatched at yy_reduce:157966 — i.e. real SQL keyword tokens (e.g. 'vacuum', ';') laid out so the LALR parser reaches that reduce rule, rather than malformed/garbage token streams that fault out earlier.
why_winner_satisfies: Both winner-resolving seeds carry literal SQL keyword bytes: Seed 1 begins ';vacuum\n\n' and Seed 2 begins 'vacuum' followed by quote/semicolon punctuation, so they tokenize into a recognized statement that drives the parser to the true side of L157966 (W: T=2 F=17).
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds are short, malformed token soup ('SELEEC .`', '""/"" ', 'T 1; c BINARY...') that never tokenize into the production reaching the reduce action — the false side dominates (L: T=0 F=131) so the true branch is never taken across naive/cmplog runs.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile adds CMP_MAP gradient feedback: each integer/string CMP records a Hamming/prefix-distance bucket, so a partial keyword match (e.g. progressively matching 'vacuum') is rewarded with a new coverage bucket and preserved as corpus. This lets value_profile / value_profile_cmplog climb toward the exact token strings the grammar needs, whereas naive and cmplog (lacking CMP_MAP) get no gradient on these keyword comparisons and stall.

what_input_feature: Input must be syntactically valid SQL (a SELECT statement) whose expression contains relational/arithmetic comparison operators (>=, <=, =, -, /) so that sqlite3GetToken classifies a character into the token class whose true branch at 159496 fires and the parser proceeds into expression evaluation.
why_winner_satisfies: The value_profile winner-resolving seed is clean SQL 'SELECT 11-013>=1...' packed with comparison/arithmetic operators (>=, <=, =, -, /); these operands feed integer-CMP callbacks whose CMP_MAP distance buckets give value_profile a coverage gradient toward valid token-producing inputs, so it resolves 9/10 trials.
why_loser_doesnt: naive's loser-blocking seeds are malformed byte soup (e.g. '<.      ;.I/;;;.' and runs of 0c/cb/df bytes) lacking a coherent SELECT/operator structure; with only edge-counter feedback and no CMP gradient, naive blocks 8/10 and reaches the branch true side far less (avg hitcount winner=3 vs loser=31 on the false side).
mechanism_attribution: value_profile intercepts integer CMPs and writes a (PC, operand-distance) bucket into CMP_MAP; an input whose comparison operands are closer to matching adds a new bucket and is kept as corpus, giving a similarity gradient that steers toward the structured SQL+operator inputs that drive the GetToken true branch. naive has no such CMP feedback. Mechanism = CMP_MAP gradient.

what_input_feature: A token whose first bytes form the SQL hex-integer prefix: z[0]=='0' AND (z[1]=='x' OR z[1]=='X') AND z[2] is a hex digit, i.e. the literal '0x' (or '0X') immediately followed by [0-9a-fA-F].
why_winner_satisfies: Every value_profile_cmplog winning seed contains an explicit '0x' hex prefix (e.g. 'SELECT-0x85E', 'SELECT -0x11CCC1', 'SELECT -0x111111', 'SELECT -0xC11180'), so z[0]=='0', z[1]=='x', z[2] hex digit all hold and the true (hex-integer) side of line 159595 is taken.
why_loser_doesnt: The cmplog loser-blocking seeds carry decimal digit runs without an 'x'/'X' after a '0' (e.g. '00S', 'SELECT.5-5/55555', 'SELECT.5E1-55'); z[1] is never 'x'/'X', so the (z[1]=='x'||z[1]=='X') sub-condition at 159595:25 evaluates false and the true side is never reached.
mechanism_attribution: Both involved fuzzers carry the same input-to-state substitution mutator; the decisive delta is value_profile. value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient buckets reward partial Hamming/prefix progress on the chained byte comparisons (z[0]=='0', z[1]=='x'/'X', isxdigit(z[2])), guiding the corpus to assemble the multi-byte '0x' prefix that pure cmplog (edge-bucket-only feedback) cannot reward incrementally.

what_input_feature: Input bytes at the tokenizer cursor that satisfy the true-side character-class / keyword comparison at sqlite3GetToken line 159623, e.g. an identifier/keyword character run such as 'SELECT', 'CEXT', or a numeric-operator expression, rather than arbitrary punctuation/whitespace bytes.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile-stored winner seeds carry recognizable SQL token fragments ('SELECT 11-1133=13...', 'CEXT 1', long uppercase-letter runs) and drive the true side (L159623 W=T:4 vs L=T:0); the winner then reaches deep expression-resolution code (walkExpr, resolveExprStep) the loser never touches.
why_loser_doesnt: naive loser-blocking seeds are scattered punctuation/whitespace-heavy bytes ('T 1; c BINARY', ';$..C..C', leading ' ;.....') that never match the token-class comparison, so L159623 stays false (T:0 F:78) and downstream expression walkers have zero invocations.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile adds a CMP_MAP keyed by (PC, operand-distance bucket): each tokenizer character/keyword CMP that gets closer to matching adds a new bucket and the near-miss input is preserved, giving a Hamming/prefix-distance gradient that ratchets bytes toward a valid token; naive has only edge counters and no such gradient, so it must hit the exact token by blind havoc.

what_input_feature: A well-formed SQL comparison expression whose operator parses to the TK_GE token (the two-character '>=' sequence) inside an expression that reaches resolveExprStep, so the switch( pExpr->op ) dispatches to the case TK_GE arm at line 98664.
why_winner_satisfies: The value_profile winner-resolving seed is 'SELECT 11-013>=1 3=13...' — a dense, valid SQL expression that literally contains the '>=' (0x3e 0x3d) operator at offsets 0x0d-0x0e and is otherwise composed of digits, '-', '=', '<=', '/' that form parseable comparison expressions; the per-role overlay marks line 98664 [B]/[W] and DIVERGENT BRANCHES shows the winner takes case TK_GE T=1.
why_loser_doesnt: The naive loser-blocking seeds contain comparison operators only sparsely and malformed ('SELECT ( 111111,...', 'SELECT+SE+...', 'SELECT ~~~~~44<4...'): they produce TK_LT ('<') or row-value parens/commas but never assemble a valid '>=' expression, so DIVERGENT BRANCHES shows the loser takes case TK_GE T=0 F=593 (never).
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's integer-CMP interception writes a CMP_MAP bucket keyed by operand-distance: as the SQL tokenizer/parser compares input characters against operator-token codepoints and the parser state machine compares pExpr->op against TK_GE, value_profile's Hamming/prefix-distance feedback rewards inputs that get progressively closer to forming the '>=' token in a comparison context, giving a gradient that naive's plain edge-counter feedback cannot follow toward the rare TK_GE dispatch.

what_input_feature: A relational-operator character ('>' or '<') in sqlite3GetToken's lexer stream followed immediately by '=', so the true branch at 159511 fires (the second-character check that extends a single-char operator into the two-char form >= / <=).
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile winner seeds carry SQL with adjacent '>'+'=' pairs: seed 0fadca0bde089a74 is literal `SELECT 11-013>=13=13...` containing `>=` and `<=`, and seeds c30df6313b55abb4 / ee8e63139c7a9d2c begin with bytes 3e 3d ('>='). The branch true side at L159511 is taken in W (T=3 F=0) but never in L (T=0 F=12).
why_loser_doesnt: naive loser seeds put '>' next to ';' rather than '=' (e.g. byte runs `3e 3b 3b 3e 3b 3b` = `>;;>;;`, and the `vacuum` seed has no operator at all), so the second-char-is-'=' test never succeeds and the true branch stays blocked across all 10 trials.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient: the tokenizer's character comparison against '=' is intercepted, and an input whose byte is operand-closer to '=' than any seen before adds a new CMP_MAP distance bucket and is preserved, climbing the gradient until the '=' lands right after '>'/'<'. naive has only edge-hit feedback and no such operand-distance signal, so it cannot steer toward the exact adjacent '=' byte.
